name: rasa.domain.core
description:
RasaOS Domain · Core. The unified canonical template every domain Element follows. Locked v1.0.0 shape (Phase 2 complete) + v1.1.0 universal starter layer: canon-required files (§4) + bin/init + bin/check-manifest + Apache-2.0 LICENSE + content/SHAPE.md documenting the shape patterns (toolkit/structural/hybrid) + a domain-agnostic opt-in starter set under content/ — meta-skills (new-skill, codify, sync), capability skills (onboard, handoff), universal rules (contract-rules, output-rules), and README conventions for skills/rules/agents. The starter set references only the Element contract (lockfile, pinned_sha, overrides, install policies) — zero subject knowledge. All opt-in: nothing installs into a consumer project until a fork flips the policy.

Fork this to author a new domain. Decide upfront which shape pattern fits — toolkit (skills/rules/agents like rasa.domain.code), structural (firm/drives/members like rasa.domain.legal), or hybrid. Keep the starter files that fit, delete the rest, add your own. Delete README.md + SHAPE.md from your fork; replace with your domain's own content/README.md. The Element ships zero domain knowledge — only the universal shape + subject-neutral starters.

Distinct from rasa.core (kind: core, the L1 shared bones every Element depends on). domain-core is the template for the domain KIND; rasa.core is the L1 dependency. Different concerns; both named 'core' for canonical positioning.

rasa.note: Canonical domain template per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md v1.3+ §4 (required files) + §7 (install policies). v1.0.0 is the locked-down shape: forks may pin to this version and absorb later minor bumps as additive. shape_pattern='agnostic' here flags that this template does not commit to toolkit/structural/hybrid; forks set their own. See content/SHAPE.md for the choice.

element.description: Files mirrored into the install target per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md §7. As of v1.1.0 the template ships a domain-agnostic starter set under content/ — all registered here as opt-in: covered by check-manifest, but NOT installed automatically. Forks change the policy to directory-mirror/file-replace as they adopt/extend content/.

files.note: Fork-time guide to the v1.1.0 universal starter layer — what ships, why it's domain-agnostic, how forks adopt/extend/delete it. Not installed into consumer projects. Forks delete this + SHAPE.md and replace with their own content/README.md.

Fork-time guide explaining toolkit vs structural shape patterns. Not installed into consumer projects. Forks should delete this from their copy and replace with content/README.md describing what's actually shipped.

Universal starter skills (v1.1.0): new-skill, codify, sync, onboard, handoff, update-docs + README conventions. Domain-agnostic — they reference only the Element contract (lockfile, pinned_sha, overrides, install policies), zero subject knowledge. Forks keep what fits, delete the rest, add their own, and flip policy to directory-mirror to install into consumer projects.

Universal starter rules (v1.1.0): contract-rules.md (Element <-> consumer discipline), output-rules.md (house style) + README conventions. Canonical install target is .claude/ root (rules install flat per domain-code precedent). Forks add subject rules here + flip policy.

Subagent scaffold + README conventions. Ships no starter agents by design — subagents are inherently subject-specific, so there's no domain-agnostic one worth shipping. Forks add agent .md files here + flip policy to directory-mirror.

seed.description: One-time files copied at install only. Per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md §7.

files.note: Per-project Claude session contract. Lives in install-target root; project-owned forever. Placeholder-free (Element identity lives in .claude/rasa.lock.json — current install-policy taxonomy doesn't substitute placeholders in skip-if-exists files; flagged in v0.1.0 AUDIT).

Stamped with the Element HEAD commit SHA at init time. Lockfile shape per canon Spec §6 (Connection Contract). Substitutes {{ELEMENT_NAME}}, {{ELEMENT_KIND}}, {{ELEMENT_VERSION}}, {{ELEMENT_REPO}}, {{ELEMENT_BRANCH}}, {{ELEMENT_SHA}}, {{TODAY}} placeholders via bin/init's init-only-with-sha handler.

scaffold.description: Empty directories created at init. v1.0.0 ships none — forks add as needed.
